Mar 2020–Feb 2024

Médico interno, Korea University Medicine

1. Práctica en salud pública y medicina preventiva
Contribuí a iniciativas nacionales de promoción de la salud y prevención de enfermedades, centrándome en programas de salud laboral y en la vigilancia epidemiológica. Apoyé los reconocimientos médicos nacionales de los trabajadores ganaderos (2022) y propuse reformas del sistema de guardias consecutivas de 36 horas para los médicos residentes (2023). Adquirí experiencia práctica en las políticas y los programas nacionales de servicios de salud mental mediante una comisión de servicio en el National Center for Mental Health (2023); comisión de servicio, Department of Mental Health Services, National Center for Mental Health, Jan 2023 – Feb 2023.
2. Investigación en medicina de salud pública
Investigo los determinantes sociales de la salud, la salud mental pública y el bienestar en el trabajo. Dirigí o colaboré en estudios sobre la jornada laboral de los médicos residentes, la COVID-19 y la equidad en salud, y análisis espaciotemporales de enfermedades. Presenté trabajos en congresos de la Korean Society for Preventive Medicine, la KAHPM y la KAMS.
3. Práctica clínica general
Adquirí experiencia clínica en atención primaria, medicina de urgencias y cuidados posoperatorios. Ejercí como médico responsable de la atención a pacientes en el Community Treatment Centre de Seúl durante la pandemia de COVID-19 (2022); médico, Seoul COVID-19 Community Treatment Center (a tiempo parcial), Jan 2022 – Jun 2022.
4. Docencia y enseñanza
Como profesor ayudante en la Korea University College of Medicine, apoyo la docencia de asignaturas de Medicina Preventiva, Derecho Médico y prácticas clínicas optativas de base comunitaria. Organizo las Preventive Medicine Grand Rounds para fomentar el aprendizaje interactivo y reforzar la formación de posgrado.

Seminario Korean Healthcare Policy 101 — seminario de posgrado, 16 semanas. Público objetivo: estudiantes de posgrado y profesionales del ámbito sanitario. title: Docencia
text:
Programa — Korean Healthcare Policy 101 (16 semanas)

Semana 1 — Orígenes y contradicciones del sistema de salud coreano: ¿es sostenible el Seguro Nacional de Salud, que se prevé que entrará en déficit antes que el sistema nacional de pensiones?
Semana 2 — Introducción a los sistemas sanitarios comparados: comparación internacional de los principales sistemas e indicadores de salud.
Semanas 3–4 — Seguro Nacional de Salud (financiación, mancomunación de riesgos, tablas de tarifas, sistemas de pago) y seguro de cuidados de larga duración.
Semana 5 — Sistema sanitario ①: atención primaria, gestión de enfermedades crónicas, sistema de médico de cabecera, atención integrada.
Semana 6 — Sistema sanitario ②: medicina de urgencias, pediatría, obstetricia, cuidados intensivos («servicios médicos esenciales»).
Semanas 7–8 — Sistema sanitario ③: sanidad pública, desigualdades en salud, sanidad regional, políticas/entornos sociales y salud, gasto sanitario catastrófico.
Semana 9 — Sistema sanitario ④: prevención del suicidio, salud mental pública, sistema de atención en salud mental.
Semana 10 — Sistema sanitario ⑤: respuesta ante enfermedades infecciosas y desastres, salud global, salud vinculada a la unificación, sistema sanitario militar.
Semana 11 — Política de recursos humanos en salud ①: estructura del personal hospitalario, educación y formación médica (PGME, CME), condiciones laborales, Medical Residents Act, Nursing Act, proyecciones de oferta/demanda (cupos de las facultades de medicina).
Semana 12 — Política de recursos humanos en salud ②: asistentes médicos (PA), ajuste del ámbito de ejercicio profesional, unificación médica, litigios médicos y responsabilidad jurídica, inmigración de médicos y enfermeros.
Semana 13 — Política de recursos humanos en salud ③: sindicatos de trabajadores sanitarios — principales actividades y cuestiones de política (acciones reivindicativas, dimisión masiva de residentes).
Semana 14 — Industria sanitaria, regulación y política legislativa.
Semana 15 — El proceso de elaboración de políticas para los profesionales: Asamblea Nacional, gobierno y sociedad civil.
Semana 16 — Conclusión y evaluación: retos y orientaciones futuras / examen final (posgrado).

title: Minku Kang강민구
text:
Minku Kang강민구


J’étudie comment le travail, les inégalités et les politiques sociales façonnent la santé mentale — et je traduis les données probantes en réformes.





Médecin spécialiste en médecine préventiveCommandement médical des forces armées, Séoul






Minku Kang est médecin spécialiste en médecine préventive et épidémiologiste social. Ses recherches interrogent la manière dont le travail précaire, les longues heures de travail et les inégalités dégradent la santé mentale — et la façon dont ces données probantes peuvent nourrir la réforme institutionnelle.


En tant que président de la Korean Intern Resident Association, il a mené la réforme nationale de la réglementation du temps de garde des internes et résidents, ce qui lui a valu une distinction du ministre de la Santé et du Bien-être. Il achève un doctorat en santé publique à Korea University et un master en politiques publiques à la KDI School, et exerce comme épidémiologiste et officier chargé des politiques de santé au Commandement médical des forces armées.




Sélection de travaux


2022–25Réforme du système de gardes de 36 heures consécutives des internes et résidentsChercheur principal 2026–Déterminants de la santé mentale chez les militairesChercheur principal 2023Inégalités de genre en santé mentale à l’ère de la pandémieJ. Prev. Med. Public Health 2026Résultats du dépistage national des cancers gastro-intestinauxBMJ Open




Axes de recherche

Médecine préventive clinique, évaluation des risques pour la santé, dépistage et prise en charge, médecine des populations et médecine du travail, médecine du mode de vie.




Déterminants sociaux de la santé


Santé mentale au travail, prévention du suicide, épidémiologie sociale, marchés du travail, inégalités de santé et environnement du quartier.




Politique et gestion de la santé


Personnel de santé, systèmes de santé et réforme des soins de santé.

title: 인터뷰: 예방의학과 공중보건 바로 알리기
text:
예방의학은 건강증진, 질병 예방, 인구집단과 개인의 건강 관리를 중점적으로 다루는 의학의 한 분야입니다.



예방의학과 전문의는 급성·만성질환에 대한 약물 처방, 의학적 검사, 기본적인 의료 술기 등 일차의료 영역의 역할을 수행할 수 있을 뿐 아니라, 간호사·사회복지사·산업위생사·영양사 등으로 구성된 다학제 보건의료팀을 이끄는 리더로서 활동합니다. 또한 병·의원, 지역사회, 산업현장 등 다양한 영역에서 사회적 자원과 보건의료 자원을 연계·통합하고, 데이터를 기반으로 질병의 예방과 관리, 건강증진을 포괄하는 인구집단 관점의 통합적 의료서비스를 제공하는 전문가입니다.



국가건강검진, 일반인구 건강위험도평가, 예방관리계획 수립(심뇌혈관질환 등), 생활습관 상담(금연, 금주, 식이, 운동 등), 사후관리 연계, 추적관리 등
직업 및 환경의학: 기업체 산업보건의, 사업장 건강관리, 환경위해요인 관리, 업무적합성 평가, 업무상질병판정위원회 참여 등
지역사회의학: 포괄적 노인평가 및 노쇠 예방, 만성질환 관리, 고위험군 방문진료, 사회적 처방을 통한 다분야 연계 등
학교보건서비스: 학생건강검진, 건강증진 및 질병예방, 환경위해요인 관리 등
여행의학, 예방접종, 예방적 약 복용 등
항공우주의학





병원 내 전략적 보건의료 리더십을 바탕으로 한 정책 수립
병원 내 경영지표, 진료현황 분석 및 적정진료 관리
환자안전사고 예방, 의료의 질 평가, 의료관련감염 예방 등





국가 및 지역사회 건강정책 수립
감염병 예방 및 관리, 재난 대응: 법정감염병 예방 및 관리, 신종감염병 대응체계 구축, 국가 재난 상황에서의 공중보건 대응
만성질환 예방 및 관리: 암 예방 및 관리, 심뇌혈관질환 등 주요 만성질환 예방 및 관리
정신건강 예방 및 관리: 자살 예방, 포괄적 공중정신건강(Public Mental Health) 관점의 정신건강 증진, 사회적 지지체계·교육·고용·주거 등 다분야 통합 접근
보건의료정책 연구·개발·자문: 입법 및 행정 분야 전문위원 활동
기초 연구 및 조사 수행: 국가 행정자료 및 빅데이터 분석, 근거 기반 정책 수립 지원, 지역사회건강조사 등 수행

section: 2. 예방의학을 왜 전공하였나요? 앞으로 무엇을 하고 싶은가요?
text:
2. 예방의학을 왜 전공하였나요? 앞으로 무엇을 하고 싶은가요?

가. 공부의 필요성
안녕하세요. 저는 대학 시절 우연한 계기를 통해 다양한 학문적 호기심을 갖게 되었고, 의학 외에도 사회학, 경제학, 정치학, 정책학 등 여러 분야를 공부하고 학위를 취득할 기회를 얻었습니다. 또한 학부생 시절부터 여러 조직에서 책임 있는 역할을 맡으며, 입법·정책 분야를 비롯한 다양한 사회적 경험을 통해 삶과 건강을 바라보는 사회적 관점에 대해서도 깊이 고민하게 되었습니다. 이러한 경험은 제가 예방의학과 공중보건의 의미를 이해하는 데 중요한 바탕이 되었습니다.
환자 한 명 한 명을 진료하는 일은 물론 매우 뜻깊습니다. 그러나 더 많은 사람들의 삶과 건강에 긍정적인 영향을 미치기 위해서는 학문적 근거에 기반한 정책적·인구집단적 접근 또한 필요하다고 생각합니다. 실제로 이를 뒷받침하는 논의도 꾸준히 축적되어 왔습니다. 예를 들어 Lalonde Report(1974)는 건강이 단순한 생리적 요인에 의해서만 결정되는 것이 아니라 사회적·심리적·물리적 환경, 보건의료제도, 생활습관 등에 크게 좌우된다고 제시하였고, 이러한 문제의식은 이후 Ottawa Charter로도 이어졌습니다. 이는 예방의학과 공중보건에만 국한되는 시각이 아니라, 어느 의학 분과를 전공하더라도 발전을 위해 반드시 필요한 관점이라고 생각합니다.
예방의학과 공중보건은 국가 공동체의 운영과 시민 건강 증진을 위해 매우 중요한 역할을 하는 분야라고 생각합니다. 특히 저출산과 고령화가 빠르게 진행되는 한국 사회에서 사회지출 전반이 급격히 증가하는 상황입니다. 이러한 배경 속에서 국가의 지속 가능성을 위해서라도 예방의학과 공중보건의 역할이 더욱 중요해지고 있습니다. 노인의 예방적 건강관리, 통합돌봄, 일차의료 기반의 만성질환 관리, 정신건강의 예방적 접근 등 여러 세부 영역에서 보다 적극적인 투자와 발전이 필요하다고 생각합니다. 이 분야는 때로 개인적 성취가 두드러져 보이는 영역은 아닐 수 있습니다. 그럼에도 규모가 크지 않은 분야일수록 누군가는 꾸준히 관심을 가지고 실무와 연구를 이어가야만 정책과 학문이 발전할 수 있다고 생각합니다.


나. 호기심과 즐거움
저는 좋아하는 공부를 지속하고, 삶의 의미를 스스로 만들어 가며, 개인적·사회적 차원에서 건강한 관계와 사회적 자본을 쌓아가는 것이 중요하다고 생각합니다. 비교적 이른 시기에 인생이 유한하다는 점을 깊이 생각하게 되었고, 그 이후로는 제게 의미가 있으면서도 사회적으로도 가치 있는 일을 해보고 싶다는 마음을 가지고 살아왔습니다.
제게 예방의학과 공중보건은 의학의 한 세부 분야이면서도 학문적으로 매우 흥미로운 영역입니다. 이 분야는 건강을 개인의 문제가 아니라 사회, 제도, 환경과 연결해서 이해하게 해주며, 사회정의와 형평성에 관한 논의와도 자연스럽게 맞닿아 있습니다. 따라서 경제학·사회학·보건학·철학 분야 등에서 세계적으로 저명한 학자들이 전개하고 있는 사회과학의 최전선 논의와도 자연히 접점을 가질 수밖에 없다는 점이 저는 예방의학과 공중보건의 큰 매력이라고 생각합니다. 비록 제가 위 학문 분야 모두의 중심에 서는 사람은 아닐지라도, 사회와 건강이라는 접점을 통해 중요한 질문들을 함께 고민할 수 있다는 점 자체가 제게는 큰 의미이자 즐거움입니다.

title: 강민구Minku Kang
text:
강민구Minku Kang


노동과 불평등이 정신건강에 미치는 영향을 연구하고, 그 근거로 더 나은 제도를 만들고자 합니다.





예방의학과 전문의국군의무사령부, 서울






예방의학과 전문의이자 사회역학 연구자입니다. 불안정한 일자리, 긴 노동시간, 지역 간 격차와 같은 사회적 조건이 사람들의 정신건강에 어떤 영향을 미치는지 연구하며, 특히 노동과 정신건강, 그리고 이를 둘러싼 보건의료정책에 관심을 두고 있습니다.


대한전공의협의회장으로서 전공의 근로시간 제도 개선을 이끌었고, 그 공로로 보건복지부장관 표창을 받았습니다. 고려대학교에서 보건학 박사과정을 수료하고 KDI 국제정책대학원에서 정책학 석사과정을 마쳤으며, 현재 국군의무사령부에서 예방의학장교·적정진료관리장교로 복무하고 있습니다.




주요 활동


2022–25전공의 36시간 연속근무 제도 개선 연구연구책임자 2026–군 장병 정신건강의 결정요인 연구연구책임자 2023코로나19 시기 정신건강의 젠더 불평등J Prev Med Public Health 2026국가 위장관암 검진 프로그램의 성과BMJ Open




관심분야




예방의학


임상예방의학, 건강위험평가, 건강검진·건강관리, 인구집단의학, 직업환경의학, 생활습관의학.




건강의 사회적 결정요인


사업장 정신건강, 자살예방, 사회역학, 노동시장, 건강불평등, 지역·근린환경.




보건정책·관리


보건의료인력, 보건의료체계, 의료개혁.

text: 삶의 즐거움을 위하여 취향과 철학을 가꾸고자 노력하고 있습니다. 커피, 음악, 공간, 책, 운동, 맑은 하늘을 좋아합니다.

title: 취미생활
text: 삶의 즐거움을 위하여 취향과 철학을 가꾸고자 노력하고 있습니다. 커피, 음악, 공간, 책, 운동, 맑은 하늘을 좋아합니다.

title: 취미생활
text:
2) 정신건강과 웰니스
정신건강을 챙기는 행복한 삶을 지향합니다. 운동은 주5일 합니다. 러닝, 축구, 필라테스, 웨이트 등 가리지 않고 꾸준히 하고 있습니다. 수면, 식사 등 웰니스에도 관심이 많습니다.
햇살 좋은 광장에서 오후 2시에 커피를 한 잔 하고, 한강에서 노을을 보면서 노을지기 전의 햇살을 캐치하는 여유로운 마음으로 하루를 마무리하는 가을방학 같은 삶을 꿈꾸고 있습니다.

title: 취미생활
text:
3) 공간, 사진, 영상, 음악
도시 곳곳을 돌아다니며 공간을 탐방합니다. 사진과 영상을 남기는 것을 좋아합니다. 영화는 주로 드라마 장르를 좋아합니다. 옷 입는 것에도 약간 관심이 있으며, 주로 올블랙을 선호합니다.
그루브 있는 음악이라면 록, 전자음악, 재즈, R&B, 인디 등 가리지 않고 좋아하며, 매년 록 페스티벌을 즐기는 편입니다. 이전에 밴드 활동하며 베이스기타 연주를 했었습니다.

좋아하는 음악

Electronic / French touch / Minimal / Synth-based — Kraftwerk, Dabeull, Breakbot, Air, Paradis, FKJ, Polo & Pan, L’Impératrice
Funk / Groove-based Indie / Nu-disco / Neo-soul — Vulfpeck, Jungle, Parcels, Papooz, Leisure, Tom Misch, Men I Trust, Rhye, The Internet, Benny Sings
Alternative / Rock / Funk-rock — Oasis, Liam Gallagher, Beck, Red Hot Chili Peppers

Jazz / Contemporary Jazz / Jazz-fusion — Keith Jarrett, Pat Metheny Group, Brad Mehldau, Esbjörn Svensson Trio, Bill Evans

title: 취미생활
text:
4) 사회 변화에 대한 기여
사회 변화에 기여할 수 있는 삶을 살려고 합니다. 저는 서로를 돌보고, 도전을 장려하며, 실패하더라도 감당할 수 있는 기반을 가진 지속가능한 공동체가 필요하다고 생각합니다. 자살률, 합계출산율, 산업재해 사망자 수와 같은 지표는 우리 사회의 구조적 문제를 보여주며, 사회적 결정요인(social determinants)과 깊이 연결되어 있습니다. 앞으로 사회적 불평등을 완화하여 더 행복하고 안전하며 지속가능한 사회를 만드는 데 기여하는 삶을 살아가고자 노력하고자 합니다.

1. Praxis in Public Health und Präventivmedizin
Mitwirkung an nationalen Initiativen zur Gesundheitsförderung und Krankheitsprävention mit Schwerpunkt auf betrieblichen Gesundheitsprogrammen und epidemiologischer Überwachung. Unterstützung der landesweiten Gesundheitsuntersuchungen für Beschäftigte in der Tierhaltung (2022) und Ausarbeitung von Reformvorschlägen für das System der durchgehenden 36-Stunden-Dienste für Assistenzärzte (2023). Erwerb praktischer Erfahrung mit nationaler Politik und Versorgungsprogrammen im Bereich der psychischen Gesundheit durch eine Abordnung an das National Center for Mental Health (2023); Abordnung, Department of Mental Health Services, National Center for Mental Health, Jan 2023 – Feb 2023.
2. Forschung im Bereich Public Health
Forschung zu sozialen Determinanten der Gesundheit, öffentlicher psychischer Gesundheit und Wohlbefinden am Arbeitsplatz. Leitung von bzw. Mitwirkung an Studien zu den Dienstzeiten von Assistenzärzten, zu COVID-19 und gesundheitlicher Chancengleichheit sowie zu raumzeitlichen Krankheitsanalysen. Präsentation von Arbeiten auf Tagungen der Korean Society for Preventive Medicine, der KAHPM und der KAMS.
3. Allgemeine klinische Tätigkeit
Erwerb klinischer Erfahrung in der Primärversorgung, der Notfallmedizin und der postoperativen Versorgung. Tätigkeit als behandelnder Arzt im Seoul Community Treatment Centre während der COVID-19-Pandemie (2022); Arzt, Seoul COVID-19 Community Treatment Center (Teilzeit), Jan 2022 – Jun 2022.
4. Lehre und Ausbildung
Als wissenschaftlicher Assistent (Teaching Assistant) am Korea University College of Medicine Mitbetreuung von Lehrveranstaltungen in Präventivmedizin, Medizinrecht und gemeindebasierten klinischen Wahlfächern. Organisation der Preventive Medicine Grand Rounds zur Förderung interaktiven Lernens und zur Stärkung der Ausbildung auf Graduiertenebene.

Kursleitung (geplant)

Seminar Korean Healthcare Policy 101 — Graduiertenseminar, 16 Wochen. Zielgruppe: Graduierte und Fachkräfte im Gesundheitswesen. Schwerpunkte: Ursprünge des koreanischen Gesundheitssystems, nationale Krankenversicherung und Langzeitpflege, Primärversorgung und Management chronischer Erkrankungen, essenzielle medizinische Leistungen, Public Health und gesundheitliche Ungleichheiten, öffentliche psychische Gesundheit, Katastrophenschutz, Personalpolitik im Gesundheitswesen sowie der gesetzgeberische Politikprozess.
Öffentliche psychische Gesundheit: Soziale Determinanten, Politik und psychische Gesundheit — Graduiertenkurs
Präventivmedizin und Public Health — Fachkurs im grundständigen Studium (Medizin)
Soziale Determinanten und Gesundheit — allgemeinbildender Kurs im grundständigen Studium

title: Lehre
text:
Lehrplan — Korean Healthcare Policy 101 (16 Wochen)

Woche 1 — Ursprünge und Widersprüche des koreanischen Gesundheitssystems: Ist die nationale Krankenversicherung, die voraussichtlich schneller als die nationale Rentenversicherung in ein Defizit gerät, nachhaltig finanzierbar?
Woche 2 — Einführung in vergleichende Gesundheitssysteme: internationaler Vergleich wichtiger Systeme und Gesundheitsindikatoren.
Wochen 3–4 — Nationale Krankenversicherung (Finanzierung, Risikoausgleich, Gebührenordnungen, Vergütungssysteme) und Langzeitpflegeversicherung.
Woche 5 — Gesundheitssystem ①: Primärversorgung, Management chronischer Erkrankungen, Hausarztsystem, integrierte Versorgung.
Woche 6 — Gesundheitssystem ②: Notfallmedizin, Pädiatrie, Geburtshilfe, Intensivmedizin („essenzielle medizinische Leistungen“).
Wochen 7–8 — Gesundheitssystem ③: öffentliche Gesundheitsversorgung, gesundheitliche Ungleichheiten, regionale Gesundheitsversorgung, Sozialpolitik/soziale Umfelder und Gesundheit, katastrophale Gesundheitsausgaben.
Woche 9 — Gesundheitssystem ④: Suizidprävention, öffentliche psychische Gesundheit, System der psychischen Gesundheitsversorgung.
Woche 10 — Gesundheitssystem ⑤: Reaktion auf Infektionskrankheiten und Katastrophen, globale Gesundheit, Gesundheitsversorgung im Kontext der Wiedervereinigung, militärisches Gesundheitssystem.
Woche 11 — Personalpolitik im Gesundheitswesen ①: Personalstruktur der Krankenhäuser, medizinische Aus- und Weiterbildung (PGME, CME), Arbeitsbedingungen, Medical Residents Act, Nursing Act, Angebots- und Nachfrageprognosen (Zulassungsquoten der medizinischen Fakultäten).
Woche 12 — Personalpolitik im Gesundheitswesen ②: Physician Assistants (PA), Anpassung des Tätigkeitsspektrums, Vereinheitlichung des Medizinsystems, medizinische Streitfälle und rechtliche Haftung, Zuwanderung von Ärzten und Pflegekräften.
Woche 13 — Personalpolitik im Gesundheitswesen ③: Gewerkschaften der Beschäftigten im Gesundheitswesen — wichtige Aktivitäten und politische Themen (Arbeitskampf, Massenkündigung von Assistenzärzten).
Woche 14 — Gesundheitswirtschaft, Regulierung und Gesetzgebungspolitik.
Woche 15 — Der Politikprozess für Praktiker: Nationalversammlung, Regierung und Zivilgesellschaft.
Woche 16 — Fazit und Leistungsbewertung: künftige Herausforderungen und Perspektiven / (Graduierten-)Abschlussprüfung.

title: What is preventive medicine?
text: Source: https://www.theabpm.org/become-certified/exam-content/preventive-medicine-core-content-outline/

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
I. Clinical Preventive Medicine 25%
Knowledge and skills that enable preventive medicine practitioners to:

Perform/obtain comprehensive individual health history, including but not limited to lifestyle, occupation, environmental exposures, hobbies, social determinants of health, sexual health, complementary and alternative therapies.
Assess individual’s health risks based on personal, family, and exposure history, lifestyle choices, and other risk factors.
Provide evidence-based guidance, advice and/or interventions to modify, mitigate, and/or eliminate risks in order to improve health outcomes and functional status.
Assess individuals using current standards and guidelines to determine suitability for duty, return to work, school, play/leisure, travel or other essential life activities.

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
II. Public Health/Population Health Medicine 25%
Knowledge and skills that enable preventive medicine practitioners to:

Perform population or community health assessment (including defining health questions, collecting, organizing, analyzing and interpreting data) to determine potential relationships, correlations, and/or causations between factors and variables related to health outcomes.
Develop and communicate policies, guidance, advice and/or interventions to various audiences (e.g., the public, organizations, media, government, publication) to modify, mitigate, and/or eliminate risks in order to improve health outcomes.
Develop assurance plan including implementation, monitoring, and surveillance recommendations in collaboration with relevant stakeholders (e.g., policy makers, community leaders, business leaders, union representatives, military leaders).

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
III. Epidemiology, Biostatistics, and Informatics 20%
Knowledge and skills that enable preventive medicine practitioners to:

Engage in the design of scientifically valid studies to answer questions or address defined hypothesis.
Interpret and critically review research methodology and integrate study results into a scientifically sound narrative to develop conclusions and/or inform next steps.
Gather, extract, evaluate, and synthesize data and information from diverse sources to inform decisions related to both population and individual health.
Communicate scientific knowledge through memos, presentations, posters, articles, media, or other methods to inform stakeholders (e.g., decision-makers, peers, and the public).

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
IV. Environmental Medicine 15%
Knowledge and skills that enable preventive medicine practitioners to:

Perform comprehensive evaluation of the environment to identify risks to which individuals or populations are exposed.
Develop and implement plans to mitigate, manage, and/or treat individual and population health risks related to environmental factors.
Develop and inform environmental policies and regulations related to the protection of human health.
Develop incident management and response plans in collaboration with physician and non-physician professionals.
Develop and/or advocate for wellness and lifestyle initiatives to address health issues and health disparities arising from environmental conditions.

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
V. Strategic Healthcare Leadership 15%
Knowledge and skills that enable preventive medicine practitioners to:

Participate in organizational governance supporting the formulation, implementation, and evaluation of health strategy.
Participate in quality improvement programs/processes within the organization to improve health outcomes.
Evaluate health and/or safety initiatives and programs to determine the efficacy and recommend modifications.
Comply with applicable standards and regulations.

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
I. Clinical Preventive Medicine

1.01 Risk factors, signs, symptoms, and approaches to prevention related to physical, mental, and behavioral diseases and disorders, including:

1.01.01. Cardiovascular diseases (e.g., peripheral vascular disease, hypertension, Coronary Artery Disease, lipid disorders)
1.01.02. Respiratory diseases (e.g., COPD, asthma, obstructive sleep apnea, pneumoconiosis)
1.01.03. Neoplastic diseases (e.g., colorectal, breast, prostate, leukemia, skin)
1.01.04. Infectious diseases (e.g., STDs, blood borne, zoonotic, parasites, TB, hepatitis, HIV, herpes-zoster, measles, pertussis, Ebola)
1.01.05. Metabolic disorders and nutrition (e.g., metabolic syndrome, diabetes, thyroid disorder, chronic kidney disease, gout)
1.01.06. Musculoskeletal disorders (e.g., spinal pathology including degenerative joint, disc and facet disease, impingement syndrome)
1.01.07. Neurological disorders (e.g., Traumatic brain injury, syncope, CVA, GBS, multiple sclerosis, migraine, occipital neuralgia, trigeminal neuralgia, sensory neural hearing loss, radiculopathy, spinal stenosis)
1.01.08. Oral health, vision and hearing disorders (e.g., dental caries, periodontal disease, oral cancer; refractive error, glaucoma, cataracts, retinal disorders-macular degeneration, diabetic retinopathy; hearing loss, tinnitus)
1.01.09 Reproductive health disorders (e.g., menstrual disorders, preconception care, family planning, ovarian cancer, cervical cancer, uterine cancer)
1.01.10. Dermatological conditions (e.g., melanoma, herpes, impetigo)
1.01.11. Substance use disorders (e.g., opioid addiction, illicit drug use, alcohol, tobacco)
1.01.12. Psychiatric and behavioral disorders (e.g., depression, anxiety, schizophrenia, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), somatoform disorders, ADHD)

1.02. Elements of a comprehensive health assessment and medical history (e.g., immunizations, sleep, hygiene, physical fitness, diet, substance use, travel history, occupation, environmental exposures, hobbies, determinants of health, sexual health, complementary and alternative therapies, supplements)
1.03. Personal preventive plans
1.04. Appropriate screening methods published by organizations (e.g., USPSTF, American Cancer Society, CDC, medical professional societies)
1.05. Lifestyle modification, wellness programs, and nutritional approaches to the prevention of diseases and disorders
1.06. Maternal and child health (e.g., hazardous exposures for women of childbearing age, supplement use [including folic acid], gestational diabetes, genetic screening and counseling)
1.07. Genetics and genetic risk factors
1.08. Social determinants of health
1.09. Use of immunizations (efficacy, administration considerations, potential interactions, risks, special considerations)
1.10. Current standards and guidelines regarding determining suitability for duty, return to work, school, play/leisure, travel or other essential life activities (e.g., FAA criteria, ACOEM practice guidelines, ODG, CDC infections disease guidelines, concussion protocols)
1.11. Complementary and alternative medicine

title: What is preventive medicine?
section: II. Public Health/Population Health and Medicine
text:
II. Public Health/Population Health and Medicine

2.01. Community/population transmissible disease concepts (e.g., immunization, herd immunity, disease transmission)
2.02. Population density and its effects (e.g., schools, refugee camps, prisons)
2.03. Considerations for screening method selection for specific communities, populations, or special groups
2.04. Behavioral economics and behavioral change models (e.g., incentivizing health policy, stages of changes, PRECEDE-PROCEED)
2.05. Social and economic factors impacting health (e.g., family and social support, community safety, income, employment)
2.06. Considerations regarding access to care and quality of care (e.g., affordability, quality metrics, value-based metrics, efficacy and cost considerations, social norms and beliefs associated with access to care)
2.07. Population-level interventions to improve health behaviors (e.g., tobacco use, diet and exercise, alcohol and drug use, safe sex practices)
2.08. Health system approach to chronic disease management and chronic disease model
2.09. High risk vs total population interventions
2.10. Health education and promotion theories and models
2.11. Health literacy
2.12. Health disparities and health equity
2.13. Community engagement strategies and advocacy
2.14. Population-level data sources (e.g., NHANES, NHIS, BRFSS, CMS, DOL, WHO, World Data, Robert Wood Johnson Foundation)
2.15. Considerations for surveillance method selection and plan development for specific communities, populations, or special groups
2.16. Ethical concepts and principles related to public health and population medicine
2.17. Legal concepts, principles, cases and legislation related to public health and population medicine
2.18. Communication management techniques

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
III. Epidemiology, Biostatistics and Informatics

3.01. Design and Methods

3.01.01. Data sources and structures
3.01.02. Study and research design
3.01.03. Biases and control measures
3.01.04. Methods for controlling for confounding variables
3.01.05. Methods to communicate research findings and disseminate information

3.02. Statistical Analysis and Interpretation

3.02.01. Measures of central tendency
3.02.02. Tests of significance
3.02.03. Probability
3.02.04. Hypothesis testing
3.02.05. Type I and type II errors
3.02.06. Confidence intervals
3.02.07. Power
3.02.08. Sample size
3.02.09. Correlation
3.02.10. Causation and association
3.02.11. Strength of evidence
3.02.12. Measurement of effect
3.02.13. Multivariate analysis
3.02.14. Multiple regression
3.02.15. Survival analysis
3.02.16. Meta-analysis
3.02.17. Outcome measures (e.g., number needed to treat, number needed to harm, effective dose, overall survival, quality-adjusted life years)
3.02.18. Tests of clinical significance
3.02.19. Tests of statistical significance
3.02.20. Decision analysis processes, methods, and tools (e.g., decision trees, modeling, ROC curves, Bayesian)
3.02.21. Principles related to systematic review

3.03. Vital Statistics and Demographics

3.03.01. Rates and measures (e.g., infant and maternal mortality rates, surrogate markers, incidence versus prevalence)
3.03.02. Trend analysis (e.g., cluster, typical versus atypical, endemics, epidemics, and pandemics)

3.04. Prevention and Control

3.04.01. Active and passive disease surveillance tools and methods
3.04.02. Screening tests (e.g., specificity v. sensitivity, positive v. negative predictive value)
3.04.03. Outbreak investigation and intervention

3.05. Informatics

3.05.01. Fundamental informatics concepts, models, and theories
3.05.02 Data mining methods and techniques
3.05.03. Information resources (e.g., databases and registries, OSHA, CDC Yellow Book, EPA)
3.05.04. Electronic health records, including order entry, use in decision support, user interfaces, patient portals, information sharing, health information exchanges

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
IV. Environmental Medicine

4.01. Physiology and physiological changes related to specific environments/activities (e.g., workplace, commercial air travel, recreational diving mountain climbing)
4.02. Environmental agents, including chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, explosive, and other agents
4.03. Environmental factors related to community health (e.g., air and water quality, sanitation, heat and cold stress, food safety, household and workplace injuries, noise)
4.04. Environmental considerations regarding social determinants of health
4.05. Industrial hygiene principles, methods, tools, and techniques (e.g., exposure dose, noise, radiation, ergonomics, repetitive motion)
4.06. Surveillance methods (monitor, control, and prevent) for infectious diseases
4.07. Principles and activities related to quarantine procedures
4.08. Principles related to interaction of exposures and individuals (e.g., toxicokinetic, toxicodynamic)
4.09. Principles and regulations and guidelines related to levels of exposure (e.g., PEL, TLV, STEL, REL)
4.10. Health conditions related to environmental factors of natural and/or man-made disasters (e.g., climate change, war, migration, resettlement)
4.11. Disaster planning and management models and policies (e.g., National Incident Management System (NIMS), Stafford Act, Defense Support to Civil Authorities, National Guards)
4.12. Methods for hazard control (e.g., engineering, administrative, personal protective measures)
4.13. Methods to assess, manage, and communicate environmental risk
4.14. General scope and impact of major environmental regulations (e.g., Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, Safe Drinking Water Act) on health and environmental medicine

title: What is preventive medicine?
text:
V. Strategic Healthcare Leadership

5.01. Health care delivery systems and governance structures
5.02. Health care financing and reimbursement systems (e.g., Medicare, insurance, alternative payment methods)
5.03. Healthcare economics concepts
5.04. Leadership principles
5.05. Interprofessional collaboration principles
5.06. Safety considerations (patient and personal)
5.07. Frameworks for program evaluation (e.g., CDC, AHRQ)
5.08. Organizational effectiveness principles
5.09. Strategic planning and policy development
5.10. Contingency planning
5.11. Quality and process improvement methods, tools, and strategies
5.12. Applicable standards and regulations (e.g., Patient protection and affordable care act, Americans with Disabilities Act, HIPAA)

title: Minku Kang강민구
text:
Minku Kang강민구


Я изучаю, как труд, неравенство и социальная политика формируют психическое здоровье, и превращаю результаты исследований в реальные реформы.





Врач профилактической медициныArmed Forces Medical Command, Сеул






Minku Kang — врач профилактической медицины и социальный эпидемиолог. Его исследования посвящены тому, как нестабильная занятость, продолжительный рабочий день и неравенство приводят к ухудшению психического здоровья и как полученные результаты могут способствовать институциональным реформам.


Будучи президентом Korean Intern Resident Association, он возглавил общенациональную работу по реформированию нормативов рабочего времени врачей-ординаторов, за что был удостоен благодарности от Minister of Health and Welfare. Он завершает докторантуру по общественному здравоохранению в Korea University и магистратуру по государственной политике в KDI School, а также служит эпидемиологом и офицером по политике здравоохранения в Armed Forces Medical Command.




Избранные работы


2022–25Реформа системы 36-часовых непрерывных дежурств ординаторовРуководитель проекта 2026–Детерминанты психического здоровья военнослужащихРуководитель проекта 2023Гендерное неравенство в психическом здоровье в период пандемииJ. Prev. Med. Public Health 2026Результаты национального скрининга рака желудочно-кишечного трактаBMJ Open




Направления исследований




Профилактическая медицина


Клиническая профилактическая медицина, оценка рисков для здоровья, скрининг и ведение пациентов, популяционная медицина и медицина труда, медицина образа жизни.




Социальные детерминанты здоровья


Психическое здоровье на рабочем месте, профилактика суицидов, социальная эпидемиология, рынки труда, неравенство в здоровье и среда проживания.




Политика и управление в здравоохранении


Медицинские кадры, системы здравоохранения и их реформирование.

専攻医（予防医学）、Korea University Medicine
インターン（研修医）、Korea University Medicine

1. 公衆衛生・予防医学実務
職域健康プログラムと疫学サーベイランスを中心に、国の健康増進・疾病予防事業に貢献した。全国の畜産従事者健康診断（2022）を支援し、専攻医の36時間連続勤務制度の改革を提言した（2023）。National Center for Mental Health への派遣（2023）を通じて、国の精神保健政策およびサービス事業に関する実務経験を積んだ。派遣：Department of Mental Health Services, National Center for Mental Health、Jan 2023 – Feb 2023。
2. 公衆衛生医学における研究
健康の社会的決定要因、公衆精神保健、職域ウェルビーイングを研究している。専攻医の勤務時間、COVID-19と健康の公平性、疾病の時空間分析に関する研究を主導し、または共同研究者として参画した。Korean Society for Preventive Medicine、KAHPM、KAMS の学術大会で研究成果を発表した。
3. 一般臨床実務
プライマリケア、救急医療、術後管理にわたる臨床経験を積んだ。COVID-19パンデミック下（2022）には、ソウルの生活治療センター（Community Treatment Centre）で患者診療医として従事した。診療医、Seoul COVID-19 Community Treatment Center（非常勤）、Jan 2022 – Jun 2022。
4. 教育・指導
Korea University College of Medicine のティーチングアシスタントとして、予防医学・医事法・地域基盤の臨床選択実習の講義を支援している。双方向的な学習を促進し、大学院水準の教育を強化するため、Preventive Medicine Grand Rounds を運営している。

title: 教育
section: シラバス — Korean Healthcare Policy 101（全16週）
text:
シラバス — Korean Healthcare Policy 101（全16週）

第1週 — 韓国の医療制度の起源と矛盾：国民年金よりも早く赤字に陥ると予測される国民健康保険制度は持続可能か。
第2週 — 比較医療制度論入門：主要な医療制度と健康指標の国際比較。
第3–4週 — 国民健康保険（財源調達、リスクプール、診療報酬体系、支払い制度）と長期療養（介護）保険。
第5週 — 医療提供体制①：プライマリ・ケア、慢性疾患管理、主治医制、統合ケア。
第6週 — 医療提供体制②：救急医療、小児科、産科、集中治療（「必須医療」）。
第7–8週 — 医療提供体制③：公共医療、健康格差、地域医療、社会政策・環境と健康、破局的医療費。
第9週 — 医療提供体制④：自殺予防、公衆メンタルヘルス、精神医療体制。
第10週 — 医療提供体制⑤：感染症・災害対応、グローバルヘルス、統一保健医療、軍の医療体制。
第11週 — 医療人材政策①：病院の人材構造、医学教育・研修（PGME、CME）、労働条件、専攻医法、看護法、需給予測（医学部定員）。
第12週 — 医療人材政策②：フィジシャン・アシスタント（PA）、業務範囲の調整、医療一元化、医療紛争と法的責任、医師・看護師の移民。
第13週 — 医療人材政策③：医療従事者の労働組合 — 主な活動と政策課題（争議行為、専攻医の集団辞職）。
第14週 — 医療産業、規制、および立法政策。
第15週 — 実務家のための政策プロセス：国会、政府、市民社会。
第16週 — まとめと評価：今後の課題と方向性／（大学院）期末試験。

Courseworks

Korean Healthcare Policy 101 Seminar — graduate seminar, 16-week. Target: healthcare-related graduate students & professionals. Highlights: origins of Korea’s health system, national health insurance & long-term care, primary care & chronic disease management, essential medical services, public health & health inequalities, public mental health, disaster response, health workforce policy, and the legislative policy process.

title: Teaching
text:
Syllabus — Korean Healthcare Policy 101 (16 weeks)

Week 1 — Origins and contradictions of Korea’s health system: is the National Health Insurance system, expected to face deficits faster than the national pension, sustainable?
Week 2 — Introduction to comparative healthcare systems: international comparison of major systems and health indicators.
Weeks 3–4 — National Health Insurance (financing, risk pooling, fee schedules, payment systems) & Long-Term Care Insurance.
Week 5 — Healthcare system ①: primary care, chronic-disease management, attending-physician system, integrated care.
Week 6 — Healthcare system ②: emergency medicine, pediatrics, obstetrics, critical care (“essential medical services”).
Weeks 7–8 — Healthcare system ③: public healthcare, health inequalities, regional healthcare, social policy/environments & health, catastrophic health expenditure.
Week 9 — Healthcare system ④: suicide prevention, public mental health, mental-healthcare system.
Week 10 — Healthcare system ⑤: infectious-disease & disaster response, global health, unification healthcare, military healthcare system.
Week 11 — Health workforce policy ①: hospital workforce structure, medical education & training (PGME, CME), working conditions, Medical Residents Act, Nursing Act, supply/demand projections (medical-school quotas).
Week 12 — Health workforce policy ②: physician assistants (PA), scope-of-practice adjustment, medical unification, medical disputes & legal liability, physician/nurse immigration.
Week 13 — Health workforce policy ③: healthcare workers’ unions — major activities and policy issues (industrial action, mass resignation of residents).
Week 14 — Healthcare industry, regulation, and legislative policy.
Week 15 — The policy process for practitioners: National Assembly, government, and civil society.
Week 16 — Conclusion & assessment: future challenges and directions / (graduate) final examination.

title: Minku Kang강민구
text:
Minku Kang강민구


労働・格差・社会政策がメンタルヘルスをどのように形づくるのかを研究し、その根拠を改革へとつなげています。





予防医学専門医国軍医務司令部、ソウル






Minku Kang は予防医学専門医であり、社会疫学者です。その研究は、不安定な労働・長時間労働・格差がどのようにメンタルヘルスの悪化を招くのか、そして、そこで得られた根拠が制度改革をいかに後押ししうるのかを問うものです。


大韓インターン・レジデント協議会の会長として、研修医の勤務時間規制改革に向けた全国的な取り組みを主導し、その功績により保健福祉部長官表彰を受けました。現在は高麗大学校で保健学の博士課程を、KDI 大学院で公共政策学の修士課程を修了しつつあり、国軍医務司令部では疫学者および保健医療政策担当官を務めています。




主な業績


2022–25研修医の36時間連続勤務制度の改革研究代表者 2026–軍人のメンタルヘルスの決定要因研究代表者 2023パンデミック下のメンタルヘルスにおけるジェンダー格差J. Prev. Med. Public Health 2026全国消化器がん検診の成果BMJ Open




重点分野




予防医学


臨床予防医学、健康リスク評価、検診・健康管理、集団医学・産業医学、生活習慣医学。




健康の社会的決定要因


職場のメンタルヘルス、自殺予防、社会疫学、労働市場、健康格差、近隣環境。




保健医療政策・管理


医療従事者、保健医療制度、医療改革。

title: Преподавание
section: Программа курса — Korean Healthcare Policy 101 (16 недель)
text:
Программа курса — Korean Healthcare Policy 101 (16 недель)

Неделя 1 — Истоки и противоречия системы здравоохранения Кореи: устойчива ли система национального медицинского страхования, которая, как ожидается, столкнётся с дефицитом быстрее, чем национальная пенсионная система?
Неделя 2 — Введение в сравнительный анализ систем здравоохранения: международное сравнение основных систем и показателей здоровья.
Недели 3–4 — Национальное медицинское страхование (финансирование, объединение рисков, тарифы на услуги, системы оплаты) и страхование долгосрочного ухода.
Неделя 5 — Система здравоохранения ①: первичная медицинская помощь, ведение хронических заболеваний, институт лечащего врача, интегрированная помощь.
Неделя 6 — Система здравоохранения ②: неотложная медицина, педиатрия, акушерство, интенсивная терапия («основные медицинские услуги»).
Недели 7–8 — Система здравоохранения ③: государственное здравоохранение, неравенство в здоровье, региональное здравоохранение, социальная политика/среда и здоровье, катастрофические расходы на медицинскую помощь.
Неделя 9 — Система здравоохранения ④: профилактика суицидов, общественное психическое здоровье, система психиатрической помощи.
Неделя 10 — Система здравоохранения ⑤: реагирование на инфекционные заболевания и чрезвычайные ситуации, глобальное здоровье, здравоохранение в контексте объединения страны, военная система здравоохранения.
Неделя 11 — Кадровая политика в здравоохранении ①: структура кадров больниц, медицинское образование и подготовка (PGME, CME), условия труда, Закон о врачах-ординаторах, Закон о сестринском деле, прогнозы спроса и предложения (квоты приёма в медицинские вузы).
Неделя 12 — Кадровая политика в здравоохранении ②: ассистенты врача (PA), корректировка сферы профессиональной деятельности, унификация медицинской системы, медицинские споры и юридическая ответственность, иммиграция врачей и медсестёр.
Неделя 13 — Кадровая политика в здравоохранении ③: профсоюзы работников здравоохранения — основные направления деятельности и вопросы политики (забастовочные действия, массовый уход врачей-ординаторов).
Неделя 14 — Индустрия здравоохранения, регулирование и законодательная политика.
Неделя 15 — Политический процесс для специалистов-практиков: Национальное собрание, правительство и гражданское общество.
Неделя 16 — Заключение и оценка: будущие вызовы и направления / (для магистрантов и аспирантов) итоговый экзамен.

Mar 2020–Feb 2024

Врач-ординатор (профилактическая медицина), Korea University Medicine
Врач-интерн, Korea University Medicine

1. Практика в области общественного здравоохранения и профилактической медицины
Участвовал в национальных инициативах по укреплению здоровья и профилактике заболеваний, уделяя особое внимание программам охраны здоровья на рабочем месте и эпидемиологическому надзору. Обеспечивал проведение национальных медицинских осмотров работников животноводства (2022) и предложил реформу 36-часового режима непрерывного дежурства для врачей-ординаторов (2023). Во время прикомандирования к National Center for Mental Health (2023) приобрёл практический опыт в сфере национальной политики и программ помощи в области психического здоровья; прикомандирование, Department of Mental Health Services, National Center for Mental Health, Jan 2023 – Feb 2023.
2. Научная работа в области общественного здравоохранения и медицины
Исследую социальные детерминанты здоровья, общественное психическое здоровье и благополучие на рабочем месте. Руководил исследованиями и участвовал в проектах, посвящённых рабочему времени врачей-ординаторов, COVID-19 и справедливости в отношении здоровья, а также пространственно-временному анализу заболеваний. Представлял работы на конференциях Korean Society for Preventive Medicine, KAHPM и KAMS.
3. Общая клиническая практика
Приобрёл клинический опыт в первичной медико-санитарной помощи, неотложной медицине и послеоперационном уходе. Во время пандемии COVID-19 работал лечащим врачом в Community Treatment Center в Сеуле (2022); врач, Seoul COVID-19 Community Treatment Center (по совместительству), Jan 2022 – Jun 2022.
4. Образование и преподавание
В качестве ассистента преподавателя в Korea University College of Medicine помогал вести курсы профилактической медицины, медицинского права и клинических факультативов на базе местных сообществ. Организовывал Preventive Medicine Grand Rounds для развития интерактивных форм обучения и укрепления подготовки на уровне аспирантуры.

title: Minku Kang강민구
text:
Minku Kang강민구


I study how work, inequality, and social policy shape mental health — and turn the evidence into reform.





Preventive medicine physicianArmed Forces Medical Command, Seoul






Minku Kang is a preventive medicine physician and social epidemiologist. His research asks how precarious work, long working hours, and inequality translate into poor mental health — and how that evidence can drive institutional reform.


As president of the Korean Intern Resident Association, he led the national effort to reform resident duty-hour regulations, for which he received a commendation from the Minister of Health and Welfare. He is completing a PhD in Public Health at Korea University and a Master of Public Policy at the KDI School, and serves as an epidemiologist and health-policy officer at the Armed Forces Medical Command.




Selected work


2022–25Reform of the resident 36-hour consecutive-duty systemPrincipal investigator 2026–Determinants of mental health among military personnelPrincipal investigator 2023Gender inequalities in pandemic-era mental healthJ. Focus




Preventive medicine


Clinical preventive medicine, health-risk assessment, screening and management, population and occupational medicine, lifestyle medicine.




Social determinants of health


Workplace mental health, suicide prevention, social epidemiology, labour markets, health inequalities, and the neighbourhood environment.




Health policy & management


Healthcare workers, health systems, and healthcare reform.

title: Minku Kang강민구
text:
Minku Kang강민구


Ich erforsche, wie Arbeit, Ungleichheit und Sozialpolitik die psychische Gesundheit prägen — und überführe die Evidenz in konkrete Reformen.





Facharzt für PräventivmedizinSanitätskommando der Streitkräfte, Seoul






Minku Kang ist Facharzt für Präventivmedizin und Sozialepidemiologe. Seine Forschung geht der Frage nach, wie sich prekäre Beschäftigung, lange Arbeitszeiten und Ungleichheit in schlechter psychischer Gesundheit niederschlagen — und wie diese Evidenz institutionelle Reformen anstoßen kann.


Als Präsident der Korean Intern Resident Association leitete er die landesweite Initiative zur Reform der Dienstzeitregelungen für Assistenzärzte, für die er mit einer Auszeichnung des Ministers für Gesundheit und Wohlfahrt geehrt wurde. Er schließt derzeit einen PhD in Public Health an der Korea University sowie einen Master of Public Policy an der KDI School ab und ist als Epidemiologe und Referent für Gesundheitspolitik am Sanitätskommando der Streitkräfte tätig.




Ausgewählte Arbeiten


2022–25Reform des 36-Stunden-Dauerdienstsystems für AssistenzärzteLeitender Forscher 2026–Determinanten der psychischen Gesundheit bei MilitärangehörigenLeitender Forscher 2023Geschlechterungleichheiten in der psychischen Gesundheit während der PandemieJ. Prev. Med. Public Health 2026Ergebnisse des nationalen Screenings auf gastrointestinale KrebserkrankungenBMJ Open




Schwerpunkte




Präventivmedizin


Klinische Präventivmedizin, Gesundheitsrisikobewertung, Screening und Management, Bevölkerungs- und Arbeitsmedizin, Lebensstilmedizin.




Soziale Determinanten der Gesundheit


Psychische Gesundheit am Arbeitsplatz, Suizidprävention, Sozialepidemiologie, Arbeitsmärkte, gesundheitliche Ungleichheiten und das Wohnumfeld.




Gesundheitspolitik und -management


Beschäftigte im Gesundheitswesen, Gesundheitssysteme und Gesundheitsreform.

Research social determinants of health, public mental health, and workplace well-being. Led or contributed to studies on medical residents’ duty hours, COVID-19 and health equity, and spatiotemporal disease analyses. Presented work at conferences by the Korean Society for Preventive Medicine, KAHPM, and KAMS.
3. General Clinical Practice
Gained clinical experience across primary care, emergency medicine, and post-operative care. Served as a patient care physician at Seoul’s Community Treatment Centre during the COVID-19 pandemic (2022); Physician, Seoul COVID-19 Community Treatment Center (part-time) Jan 2022 – Jun 2022.
4. Education & Teaching
As a teaching assistant at Korea University College of Medicine, support courses in Preventive Medicine, Medical Law, and community-based clinical electives. Organize Preventive Medicine Grand Rounds to foster interactive learning and strengthen graduate-level training.

title: 교육
text:
개설 교과목
담당 예정 강의

한국 보건의료정책 101 세미나 — 대학원 16주 과정. 대상: 보건의료정책에 관심 있는 대학원생 및 보건의료 전문가. 주요 주제: 대한민국 보건의료체계의 기원과 모순, 국민건강보험 및 장기요양보험, 일차보건의료, 필수의료, 건강불평등, 공중정신보건 및 자살예방, 감염병 및 재난 대응, 의료인력정책(전공의, 간호법 등), 국회 및 정부의 정책 실무 과정 등.
공중정신건강: 사회적 결정요인, 정책과 정신건강 — 대학원 과정
예방의학과 공중보건 — 학부 전공 (의학과)
사회적 결정요인과 건강 — 학부 교양

title: 교육
text:
강의계획서 — 한국 보건의료정책 101 (16주)

1주차 — 대한민국 보건의료체계의 기원과 모순: 연금보다 빠른 적자가 예상되는 전국민 건강보험제도, 지속 가능한가?
2주차 — 비교보건의료체계 총론: 각국 주요제도 및 건강지표 국제 비교
3~4주차 — 국민건강보험 총론(재정조달, 위험분담, 수가체계, 지불제도 등) 및 장기요양보험 총론
5주차 — 보건의료체계 ①: 일차보건의료, 만성질환 관리, 주치의제, 통합돌봄
6주차 — 보건의료체계 ②: 응급의료, 소아, 분만, 중환자 (소위 필수의료)
7~8주차 — 보건의료체계 ③: 공공보건의료, 건강불평등, 지역의료, 사회정책/사회환경과 건강, 재난적 의료비
9주차 — 보건의료체계 ④: 자살예방, 공중정신보건, 정신보건의료체계
10주차 — 보건의료체계 ⑤: 감염병 및 재난 대응, 국제보건, 통일보건의료, 군 보건의료체계
11주차 — 의료인력정책 ①: 병원 인력 구조, 의료인 교육수련(PGME, CME), 노동조건 및 처우, 전공의법, 간호법, 수급 추계 (의대정원 등)
12주차 — 의료인력정책 ②: PA, 직역 간 업무 범위 조정, 의료일원화, 의료분쟁 및 법적 책임, 의사/간호사 이민
13주차 — 의료인력정책 ③: 보건의료인 노동조합 — 주요 활동 및 정책이슈 (보건의료노조 쟁의행위, 전공의 대규모 사직 사태 등)
14주차 — 헬스케어 산업, 규제, 입법정책
15주차 — 정책실무자에게 필요한 정책과정 이해: 국회, 정부, 시민사회
16주차 — 미래 주요 과제 및 발전 방향 / (대학원) 최종시험

강민구 · 예방의학과 전문의 | MD, MPP, Ph.D. c
고려대학교에서 의학·사회학·경제학을 공부하고 동 대학원에서 보건학 박사과정을 수료하였으며, KDI 국제정책대학원에서 정책학 석사과정을 마쳤다. 예방의학과 전문의로서 현재 국군의무사령부에서 예방의학장교·적정진료관리장교로 복무 중이다. 학부 시절 고려대학교 부총학생회장으로서 청년·지역사회 거버넌스에 참여한 것을 시작으로, 공동체의 사회·제도적 문제 해결에 관심을 가져왔다. 대한전공의협의회장을 역임하며 전공의 근로환경 개선과 근로시간 제도 개편에 기여하였고, 보건복지부 보건의료정책심의위원회 전문위원·수련환경평가위원회 위원을 지내며 보건복지부장관 표창을 받았다. 현재 대한예방의학회 이사(미래세대위원회 위원장)와 대한예방의학과의사회 부회장으로 활동하고 있다. 불평등한 노동·생활 조건이 건강에 미치는 경로를 규명해 현실 문제의 해법을 찾고, 적절한 보건사회정책으로 다양한 사람들이 함께 어울려 사는 사회를 만드는 것을 지향한다.

title: Enseignement
section: Plan de cours — Korean Healthcare Policy 101 (16 semaines)
text:
Plan de cours — Korean Healthcare Policy 101 (16 semaines)

Semaine 1 — Origines et contradictions du système de santé coréen : le régime national d’assurance maladie, dont on prévoit qu’il connaîtra des déficits plus vite que le régime national de retraite, est-il viable ?
Semaine 2 — Introduction aux systèmes de santé comparés : comparaison internationale des principaux systèmes et indicateurs de santé.
Semaines 3–4 — Assurance maladie nationale (financement, mutualisation des risques, grilles tarifaires, systèmes de paiement) et assurance de soins de longue durée.
Semaine 5 — Système de santé ① : soins primaires, gestion des maladies chroniques, système du médecin traitant, soins intégrés.
Semaine 6 — Système de santé ② : médecine d’urgence, pédiatrie, obstétrique, soins intensifs (« services médicaux essentiels »).
Semaines 7–8 — Système de santé ③ : santé publique, inégalités de santé, santé régionale, politiques/environnements sociaux et santé, dépenses de santé catastrophiques.
Semaine 9 — Système de santé ④ : prévention du suicide, santé mentale publique, système de soins en santé mentale.
Semaine 10 — Système de santé ⑤ : réponse aux maladies infectieuses et aux catastrophes, santé mondiale, santé liée à la réunification, système de santé militaire.
Semaine 11 — Politique de la main-d’œuvre en santé ① : structure des effectifs hospitaliers, formation médicale (PGME, CME), conditions de travail, Medical Residents Act, Nursing Act, projections de l’offre et de la demande (quotas des facultés de médecine).
Semaine 12 — Politique de la main-d’œuvre en santé ② : assistants médicaux (PA), ajustement du champ d’exercice, unification médicale, litiges médicaux et responsabilité juridique, immigration des médecins et des infirmiers.
Semaine 13 — Politique de la main-d’œuvre en santé ③ : syndicats des travailleurs de la santé — principales activités et enjeux politiques (actions revendicatives, démission massive des résidents).
Semaine 14 — Industrie de la santé, réglementation et politique législative.
Semaine 15 — Le processus d’élaboration des politiques pour les praticiens : Assemblée nationale, gouvernement et société civile.
Semaine 16 — Conclusion et évaluation : défis et orientations à venir / examen final (cycle supérieur).

title: Minku Kang강민구
text:
Minku Kang강민구


Estudio cómo el trabajo, la desigualdad y la política social configuran la salud mental — y traduzco la evidencia en reformas.





Médico de medicina preventivaComando Médico de las Fuerzas Armadas, Seúl






Minku Kang es médico de medicina preventiva y epidemiólogo social. Su investigación examina cómo el trabajo precario, las largas jornadas laborales y la desigualdad se traducen en un deterioro de la salud mental — y cómo esa evidencia puede impulsar la reforma institucional.


Como presidente de la Korean Intern Resident Association, encabezó la iniciativa nacional para reformar la normativa sobre las horas de guardia de los médicos residentes, por lo que recibió un reconocimiento del ministro de Salud y Bienestar. Está finalizando un doctorado en Salud Pública en Korea University y un máster en Políticas Públicas en la KDI School, y ejerce como epidemiólogo y oficial de políticas de salud en el Comando Médico de las Fuerzas Armadas.




Trabajos seleccionados


2022–25Reforma del sistema de guardias consecutivas de 36 horas de los residentesInvestigador principal 2026–Determinantes de la salud mental en el personal militarInvestigador principal 2023Desigualdades de género en la salud mental durante la pandemiaJ. Prev. Med. Public Health 2026Resultados del cribado nacional de cáncer gastrointestinalBMJ Open




Líneas de investigación




Medicina preventiva


Medicina preventiva clínica, evaluación de riesgos para la salud, cribado y manejo, medicina poblacional y ocupacional, medicina del estilo de vida.




Determinantes sociales de la salud


Salud mental en el trabajo, prevención del suicidio, epidemiología social, mercados laborales, desigualdades en salud y el entorno del barrio.




Política y gestión de la salud


Personal sanitario, sistemas de salud y reforma sanitaria.
